title: Over 1,000 Civilians Killed in U.S.-Israeli Airstrikes on Iran
detail: In the first five days of the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign on Iran, over 1,000 civilians, including at least 181 children under the age of ten, have been killed, according to Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA). The deadliest incident involved a strike on the Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school in Minab, reportedly killing between 108 and over 160 children.

media.caption: Over 1,000 Civilians Killed in U.S.-Israeli Airstrikes on Iran

title: U.S. Submarine Sinks Iranian Frigate Near Sri Lanka
detail: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi confirmed that a U.S. submarine sank the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena near Sri Lanka, resulting in 87 deaths and 32 rescues. Araghchi warned that the U.S. 'will come to bitterly regret' setting such a precedent.

media.caption: U.S. Submarine Sinks Iranian Frigate Near Sri Lanka

title: Iran Launches Missile Attacks on Israel and U.S. Bases
detail: As the Middle East conflict enters its sixth day, Iran has launched more missile attacks targeting Israel and U.S. military bases. Israel intercepted the threats and intensified retaliatory strikes, including a large-scale assault on infrastructure in Tehran and continued strikes on Hezbollah positions in Lebanon.

media.caption: Iran Launches Missile Attacks on Israel and U.S. Bases

title: U.S. House to Vote on Iran War Powers Resolution
detail: The U.S. House of Representatives is preparing to vote on a war powers resolution aimed at halting President Donald Trump's military campaign against Iran. This move reflects growing congressional unease about the President's unilateral decision to initiate conflict without congressional authorization.

media.caption: U.S. House to Vote on Iran War Powers Resolution

title: Majority of Americans Disapprove of U.S.-Israeli Strikes on Iran
detail: Recent polls indicate that around 60% of Americans disapprove of the U.S.-Israeli military strikes on Iran, while only 41% approve. The conflict has resulted in the deaths of six U.S. service members, contributing to public uncertainty and opposition.

title: U.S. Asserts Air Dominance Over Iran Amid Ongoing Conflict
detail: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed that while the U.S. maintains air superiority over Iran, not all Iranian missile or drone attacks can be stopped. The U.S. has heavily invested in regional defenses, but threats remain high, especially following a deadly drone strike in Kuwait that killed six American soldiers.

media.caption: U.S. Asserts Air Dominance Over Iran Amid Ongoing Conflict

title: Iran Launches Missiles at Israel; No Casualties Reported
detail: Iran launched a round of missiles at Israel, triggering alerts in several areas including Tel Aviv. Israel's military identified the missiles and activated defensive systems to intercept the threats. There were no immediate reports of casualties.

media.caption: Iran Launches Missiles at Israel; No Casualties Reported
